summary: ACCEPT for fixed implementation commit f69882f5387693f1aa10eca1d5adfdca7b206214 after independent QA review. The implementation and current process branch diff stay inside allowed paths with no forbidden package, gate, roadmap, release, telemetry, server, multiplayer, account, cloud-save, branding, final-art, or manual-node-battle scope. Player-visible appointment flow covers office selection, candidate comparison, eligible/rejected explanation, impact preview, confirmation, and result feedback. Bulk preview is collapsed and informational in default player mode, while bulk submit and raw appointment reason details are only in the explicit debug overlay. Default player mode hides raw reason-code text, raw reason-code DOM attributes, state hash, prototype diagnostics, and the legacy M3 appointment workspace; localized reason strings are used through the UI i18n layer. Source inspection and tests support the systems_architect ACCEPT claims: the flow consumes existing read models and command builders, does not write authoritative WorldState, does not duplicate appointment/governance formulas, and preserves the standard sim.appoint-office GameCommand surface.

decisions: QA accepts the systems_architect ACCEPT claims after independent source inspection and rerunning the required UI/client/web/E2E/check/build/storybook commands.

Scope decision: the implementation commit and current process branch diff are confined to allowed UI/web/client-core test/story/process paths and do not touch forbidden architecture, simulation, protocol, save, platform, content, CI, roadmap, gate, release, telemetry, server, account, cloud-save, branding, final-art, or manual-node-battle surfaces.
Player-flow decision: default appointment UI provides office selection, candidate comparison, eligible/rejected explanation, read-model impact preview, explicit confirm, and result feedback.
Bulk/debug decision: default bulk preview is collapsed informational context and cannot submit; bulk submit remains in the explicit debug overlay only.

Localization/debug decision: default player mode uses UI-layer formatted/localized reason strings and does not expose raw reason-code text or data-reason-code attributes; raw appointment reason details are limited to the debug workspace.

Command parity decision: player confirmation submits createM3AppointmentCommand for the standard sim.appoint-office GameCommand surface; no player-only hidden command path or domain mutation was found.
Human Gate decision: no Human Gate is required for this QA acceptance; DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE remains preserved.
risks: The apps/web shell still records local UI status after command submission rather than submitting to the live simulation worker. This is pre-existing shell-fixture behavior and remains acceptable only while runtime integration continues to preserve the same GameCommand boundary.
The explicit developer overlay intentionally retains legacy technical labels, raw IDs, and raw reason details. QA acceptance is for default player-mode separation plus explicit debug affordance.
Web and Storybook builds still report existing large chunk warnings; not introduced as a blocker for this scoped appointment-flow task.
requested_action: Lead may proceed with integration/closure gates for M7-APPOINTMENT-FLOW-QUALITY-001 at fixed implementation commit f69882f5387693f1aa10eca1d5adfdca7b206214.
